title: iria mucronata
intro: Iria mucronata, also known as the "Iria" or "Habitat", is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Iria mucronata is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
